name: model_name
desc: Prefix for all output filenames. Use alphanumeric characters and underscores only.
sourceComment: Specifies the model name, which serves as the prefix for all output filenames.

name: path2output
desc: Output directory path. def_dir resolves to outputs/<model_name>/ under the working directory.
sourceComment: Defines the output directory where all generated files will be stored.

name: output_format
desc: Output format. Currently only JSON is supported.
sourceComment: Specifies the output format.

name: simplify_npoints
desc: Target number of points retained for the simplified profile arrays in each snapshot. Clamped to ≥ 20.
sourceComment: Target number of points retained for simplified profile arrays in saved snapshots (bubble_T_arr, bubble_n_arr, bubble_dTdr_arr, bubble_v_arr, shell_grav_force_m, shell_n_arr). Default 100. Larger values give higher-fidelity snapshots at the cost of larger output files. Clamped to >= 20 (matches the coverage-skeleton chunk count). The first two simplify calls per implicit-phase snapshot log their reconstruction R² at INFO level so you can verify the chosen budget is faithful.

name: log_level
desc: Logging verbosity: DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL.
sourceComment: Logging level for terminal and file output. Controls how much detail is logged.

name: log_console
desc: Enable terminal output for log messages.
sourceComment: Enable console (terminal) output for logging. If True, log messages are printed to terminal.

name: log_file
desc: Write log messages to {path2output}/trinity.log.
sourceComment: Enable file output for logging. If True, log messages are written to a .log file in the output directory.

name: log_colors
desc: Color-code terminal output by severity level.
sourceComment: Use colored output in terminal. If True, log messages are color-coded by severity (DEBUG=cyan, INFO=green, WARNING=yellow, ERROR=red, CRITICAL=magenta).

name: mCloud
desc: Total mass of the molecular cloud.
notes: Cluster mass and remaining cloud mass derive from this (× sfe and × (1 − sfe)).
sourceComment: The mass of the molecular cloud.

name: sfe
desc: Star formation efficiency (0 < sfe < 1). Fraction of cloud mass converted to stars.
notes: Drives the cluster mass and the entire feedback budget; setting sfe close to 1 leaves no gas reservoir for shell expansion.
sourceComment: Star formation efficiency.

name: ZCloud
desc: Cloud metallicity. Currently only solar (1) is supported.
notes: Sets metal-line cooling, dust opacity, and stellar wind mass-loss scaling; only solar is supported in this release.
sourceComment: Cloud metallicity

name: include_PHII
desc: Include HII pressure in P_drive. When False, P_HII is set to zero.
notes: Setting to False reproduces a WARPFIELD-style driving budget without photoionised-gas pressure, useful for comparison runs.
sourceComment: Include HII pressure (from Strömgren ionization balance in shell) in P_drive. When False, P_HII is set to zero.

name: dens_profile
desc: Density profile type: densPL (power-law) or densBE (Bonnor-Ebert).
notes: densPL covers the homogeneous and isothermal-sphere limits via densPL_alpha = 0 or −2; densBE is the Bonnor-Ebert isothermal sphere parameterised by Ω.
sourceComment: Specifies how the cloud density scales with radius.

name: densBE_Omega
desc: Density contrast Ω = ρ_center / ρ_edge. Values > 14.1 indicate gravitational instability.
sourceComment: if `densBE` is selected, then the ratio `Omega = nCore/nCloudEdge` must be specified.

name: densPL_alpha
desc: Power-law exponent (−2 ≤ α ≤ 0). 0 = homogeneous, −2 = isothermal sphere.
notes: α controls how steeply the density falls from the core outward; α = 0 is homogeneous and α = −2 reproduces an isothermal sphere.
sourceComment: if `densPL` is selected, then the power-law coefficient `nCore*(r/rCore)^alpha` (0 = homogeneous, -2 = isothermal) must be specified.

name: nCore
desc: Core number density. For homogeneous clouds (densPL_alpha=0), this is the average density.
sourceComment: Hydrogen nuclei number density of cloud core (n_H). Standard GMC/ISM convention. Mass density: rho = nCore * mu_convert * m_H. If `densPL` AND densPL_alpha = 0, this is the average cloud density.

name: nISM
desc: Ambient ISM number density.
notes: Also used by the dissolution check: the shell is considered dissolved once shell_nMax stays below nISM for stop_t_diss.
sourceComment: Hydrogen nuclei number density of the ambient ISM (n_H). Mass density: rho = nISM * mu_convert * m_H.

name: rCore
desc: Core radius. Not used for homogeneous clouds.
sourceComment: Core radius of the molecular cloud.

name: allowShellDissolution
desc: Allow shell dissolution to terminate the simulation. If False, the dissolution check is disabled.
sourceComment: Allow shell dissolution to terminate simulation. If False, shell dissolution check is disabled.

name: stop_t_diss
desc: Duration shell_nMax must remain below nISM before dissolution is triggered.
sourceComment: Duration (in Myr) that shell_nMax must remain below nISM before dissolution is triggered.

name: stop_r
desc: Maximum shell radius. Exceeding this triggers termination. Set to None to disable.
notes: Set to None to disable; the simulation then terminates only via stop_t, dissolution, or cloud-boundary conditions.
sourceComment: Maximum radial extent permitted for shell expansion. Set to None to disable this termination condition.

name: stop_v
desc: Velocity threshold for numerical instability detection.

name: stop_t
desc: Maximum simulation duration. Set to None to disable.
notes: Set to None to let the simulation evolve until dissolution or another termination condition fires.
sourceComment: Maximum duration of the simulation. Set to None to disable this termination condition.

name: stop_at_rCloud_nSnap
desc: Terminate after the shell crosses the cloud edge. None disables; 0 stops at the edge; N > 0 lets the implicit phase advance for N more snapshots before terminating.
notes: Combines with the energy-phase reconciliation snapshot and the implicit-phase end snapshot; total post-rCloud samples is roughly N + 2.
sourceComment: Terminate simulation after the shell crosses the cloud edge (R2 > rCloud). Value is the number of post-crossing segment-loop snapshots to record before terminating. Set to None to disable. 0 stops at the edge (1a reconciliation snapshot only). N>0 lets the implicit phase advance for N more segments past the crossing — note the implicit phase's end-of-phase reconciliation snapshot adds one extra past-rCloud sample, so the total snapshots with R2 >= rCloud is roughly N + 2 (1 at-edge + N in-loop + 1 recon).

name: coll_r
desc: Radius below which the cloud is considered completely collapsed.
sourceComment: Radius below which the cloud is considered completely collapsed.

name: SB99_mass
desc: Reference cluster mass used in SB99 files. Used for scaling.
sourceComment: Reference cluster mass the bundled SB99 grid is normalized against. Read at startup by sps_refmass's def_value fallback. Not used elsewhere under the bundled default.

name: SB99_rotation
desc: Include stellar rotation (1=yes, 0=no). Rotation extends lifetimes via internal mixing.
notes: Rotation extends main-sequence lifetimes via internal mixing and shifts the wind/SN feedback timeline.
sourceComment: Determines whether stellar rotation is considered in the stellar track. Read by TWO subsystems independently: (1) Legacy SB99 filename grammar (only when sps_path = def_path). (2) Non-CIE cooling-table selection (trinity/cooling/non_CIE/read_cloudy.py:47), which picks opiate_cooling_rot_* vs opiate_cooling_norot_* regardless of whether sps_path is set. So this parameter survives even after the legacy SPS grammar is removed.

name: SB99_BHCUT
desc: Black hole formation threshold. Stars above this ZAMS mass collapse directly to BH without SN.
sourceComment: Black hole formation threshold used when the SB99 grid was generated. Informational only under the bundled default (lib/default/sps/1e6cluster_default.csv is BHCUT=120).

name: sps_refmass
desc: Reference mass for feedback scaling (f_mass = mCluster / sps_refmass). def_value copies SB99_mass; override only for a custom SPS file at a different reference mass.
sourceComment: Reference cluster mass used in f_mass = mCluster / sps_refmass. If def_value (default), copied from SB99_mass at startup so the bundled lib/default/sps/1e6cluster_default.csv (built at 1e6 Msun) scales correctly. Override when sps_path points at an SPS file normalized to a different reference mass.

name: FB_mColdWindFrac
desc: Fraction of cold material swept up by protostellar winds.
sourceComment: Fraction of cold mass entrained in stellar winds (increases Mdot_wind, reduces velocity).

name: FB_mColdSNFrac
desc: Fraction of cold ejecta from supernovae.
sourceComment: Fraction of cold mass entrained in supernova ejecta (increases Mdot_SN, reduces velocity).

name: FB_thermCoeffWind
desc: Thermalization efficiency for stellar wind kinetic energy.
sourceComment: Defines the thermalization efficiency for colliding stellar winds.

name: FB_thermCoeffSN
desc: Thermalization efficiency for supernova ejecta.
sourceComment: Defines the thermalization efficiency for supernova ejecta.

name: FB_vSN
desc: Supernova ejecta velocity.
sourceComment: Specifies the velocity of supernova ejecta.

name: mu_atom
desc: Mean molecular weight of neutral atomic gas (HI + He). μ = 14/11.
sourceComment: Mean molecular weight for neutral atomic gas (HI + He). Dimensionless, in units of m_H. Composition: He:H = 1:10 by number. Formula: (10×1 + 1×4) / 11 = 14/11.

name: mu_ion
desc: Mean molecular weight of fully ionized gas (H+ + He++). μ = 14/23.
sourceComment: Mean molecular weight for fully ionized gas (H+ + He++). Dimensionless, in units of m_H. Composition: He:H = 1:10 by number. Formula: (10×1 + 1×4) / 23 = 14/23.

name: mu_mol
desc: Mean molecular weight of molecular gas (H2 + He). μ = 14/6.
sourceComment: Mean molecular weight for molecular gas (H2 + He). Dimensionless, in units of m_H. Composition: He:H = 1:10 by number. Formula: (10×1 + 1×4) / 6 = 14/6.

name: mu_convert
desc: Mass density conversion factor (n to ρ).
sourceComment: Conversion factor for mass density calculation (mass integration). Use this for n→rho conversion. Independent of ionization state - ionization changes particle counts but NOT total mass.

name: TShell_neu
desc: Neutral shell temperature.
sourceComment: Temperature of the neutral shell region.

name: TShell_ion
desc: Ionized shell temperature.
sourceComment: Temperature of the ionised shell region.

name: dust_sigma
desc: Dust cross-section at solar metallicity.
sourceComment: Dust cross-section at solar metallicity.

name: dust_noZ
desc: Metallicity below which dust is negligible.
sourceComment: Metallicity below which there is effectively no dust

name: dust_KappaIR
desc: Rosseland mean dust opacity κ_IR.
sourceComment: The Rosseland mean dust opacity kappa_IR.

name: caseB_alpha
desc: Case B recombination coefficient.
sourceComment: The case B recombination coefficient

name: gamma_adia
desc: Adiabatic index.
sourceComment: the adiabatic index

name: C_thermal
desc: Thermal conduction coefficient.
sourceComment: The thermal conduction coefficient C

name: c_light
desc: Speed of light.
sourceComment: speed of light

name: G
desc: Gravitational constant.
sourceComment: Gravitational constant

name: k_B
desc: Boltzmann constant.
sourceComment: Boltzmann constant

name: PISM
desc: ISM pressure P/k_B.
sourceComment: ISM Pressure, P/k

name: adiabaticOnlyInCore
desc: Restrict adiabatic (energy-driven) phase to within core radius.

name: immediate_leak
desc: Transition immediately to momentum-driven phase when bubble bursts.

name: phaseSwitch_LlossLgain
desc: Threshold for (L_gain − L_loss) / L_gain to trigger phase transition.
sourceComment: When (Lgain-Lloss)/Lgain approaches this value, begin momentum-driving phase.

name: use_adaptive_solver
desc: Use the adaptive ODE solver for the energy-driven phase. If False, falls back to the original solver.
notes: Adaptive is the default; the legacy fixed-step solver in run_energy_phase.py is kept for reproducibility of older runs.

name: cool_alpha
desc: Cooling parameter α = v_2 · t_now / R_2.
sourceComment: Cooling related values. alpha = v2*t_now/R2

name: cool_beta
desc: Cooling parameter β = −dP_b/dt.
sourceComment: Cooling related values. beta = - dPb/dt.

name: cool_delta
desc: Cooling parameter δ = dT/dt.
sourceComment: Cooling related values. delta = dT/dt.

name: path_cooling_CIE
desc: Cooling curve for CIE (T > 10^5.5 K). Presets: 1=CLOUDY HII, 2=CLOUDY+grains, 3=Gnat & Ferland 2012, 4=Sutherland & Dopita 0.15Z.
notes: The integer preset is a shorthand; supplying a path string instead loads a user-provided CIE cooling table.
sourceComment: Selects the CIE (T > 10^5.5 K) cooling curve. Under ZCloud == 1 (solar), this is an integer in {1, 2, 3} indexing the bundled tables: 1 -> lib/default/cooling/CIE/coolingCIE_1_Cloudy.dat 2 -> lib/default/cooling/CIE/coolingCIE_2_Cloudy_grains.dat 3 -> lib/default/cooling/CIE/coolingCIE_3_Gnat-Ferland2012.dat Under ZCloud == 0.15, this is ignored and the loader pins to lib/default/cooling/CIE/coolingCIE_4_Sutherland-Dopita1993.dat.

name: path_cooling_nonCIE
desc: Path to non-CIE cooling curves (T < 10^5.5 K).
sourceComment: Folder containing the non-CIE (T < 10^5.5 K) OPIATE/CLOUDY cubes. Sentinel 'def_dir' resolves to lib/default/cooling/opiate/, the bundled per-age cube set; filenames inside follow the OPIATE grammar opiate_cooling_{rot|norot}_Z{1.00|0.15}_age{a}.dat.

name: path_sps
desc: Path to Starburst99 stellar population files.
sourceComment: Directory anchor for SPS data. Sentinel 'def_dir' resolves to lib/default/sps/, where the bundled 1e6cluster_default.csv lives. Currently informational only — sps_path's def_path branch resolves directly to the bundled CSV without joining against path_sps.

name: sps_path
desc: Full path to an SPS data file. def_path (default) uses the bundled Starburst99 grid; any other path enables user mode, which needs sps_col_* column declarations.
sourceComment: Full path to an SPS data file. If def_path (default), resolves to the bundled lib/default/sps/1e6cluster_default.csv (an SB99 grid: rotation=1, ZCloud=1, BHCUT=120 Msun, mass=1e6 Msun, exported as CSV with the canonical 7-column layout). The bundled CSV is loaded with the LEGACY_SB99_COLUMN_MAP positional preset; the sps_col_* declarations below are NOT required for the default. Setting sps_path to any other value triggers user mode and requires sps_col_<canonical> declarations describing your file's column layout. See the worked example further down.

name: bubble_xi_Tb
desc: Relative radius ξ = r/R_2 for measuring bubble temperature.
sourceComment: The relative radius xi = r/R2, at which we measure the bubble temperature.
